data.title: Initial jobless claims unexpectedly jump in past week
data.description: Initial jobless claims for the week ended Dec. 7 rose by 17K to 242K, well above the 221K consensus and 225K prior (revised from 224K), the U.S. Department of Labor said on Thursday.

data.title: Stock index futures fall sharply on hotter than expected wholesale inflation report
data.description:
Stock index futures traded in the red on Thursday as November producer price index came in hotter than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.31%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.18%.

November Producer Price Index, or PPI came in at +0.4% M/M vs. +0.3% consensus, and +3.0% Y/Y vs. +2.6%. Core PPI came in at +0.2% M/M vs. +0.2% consensus, and +3.4% Y/Y vs. +3.2% consensus.

Initial jobless claims unexpectedly jumped in the past week.

U.S. stocks finished mostly higher in Wednesday's history-making session, as an in-line consumer inflation report bolstered expectations of another quarter-point rate cut by the Federal Reserve next week. Nasdaq had ended above 20K, with the Big Tech boosting the index.

"Markets put in a decent performance yesterday, as the U.S. CPI report came in broadly as expected, which was seen as giving the all-clear for the Federal Reserve to cut rates next week," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"When it comes to the Fed, the CPI print left little doubt among investors that another rate cut will happen next week," Allen added.

Adobe (ADBE) shares fell 10.1% in premarket trade after the Photoshop creator issued guidance for fiscal 2025 that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

More on markets

  • Behind the monster rally that drove the Nasdaq to 20,000
  • CPI Report: Inflation Is No Longer A Concern
  • Reaction Roundup: Experts weigh in on November consumer inflation report
  • The Recipe For A Bull Market

data.title: ECB cuts interest rate by 25bps as expected, fourth cut this year
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) slashed its interest rate by 25 basis points to 3.00% on Thursday as broadly priced by the market. This is the fourth cut this year.

The decision is based on its updated assessment of the inflation outlook and the strength of monetary policy transmission.

The bank outlined that its staff sees its headline inflation averaging 2.4% in 2024, 2.1% in 2025, 1.9% in 2026, and 2.1% in 2027. "Most measures of underlying inflation suggest that inflation will settle at around the Governing Council’s 2% medium-term target on a sustained basis," the bank said in a statement.

It now expects slower economic recovery than September projections.

The interest rates on the deposit facility, the main refinancing operations, and the marginal lending facility will be decreased to 3.00%, 3.15%, and 3.40%, respectively, with effect from 18 December 2024.

ETFs to track: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

data.title: Nano Labs reports ~360 bitcoin holdings as of December 12
data.description:
• China-based circuit design company Nano Labs (NASDAQ:NA) held ~360 bitcoins as of December 12, with a current value of about $36.22M.
  • The average acquisition cost of its bitcoin holdings comes to roughly $99.7K per bitcoin, inclusive of transaction fees.
  • NA shares were trading +4.25% Thursday pre-market at $8.09.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SHARC International Systems names Michael Albertson as CEO
data.description:
• SHARC International Systems (OTCQB:INTWF) said it has appointed Michael Albertson as its new CEO, president and director.
  • Albertson will succeed Lynn Mueller, who has been the firm's CEO, president and chairman since 2014.
  • Mueller will stay on as executive chairman.

data.title: 180 Life Sciences regains compliance with Nasdaq
data.description:
• 180 Life Sciences (NASDAQ:ATNF) said it has regained compliance with Nasdaq's listing rule.
  • The firm was non-compliance with Nasdaq's rule of maintaining an audit committee consisting of three independent directors.

data.title: UBS downgrades Steel Dynamics, Nucor and Commercial Metals Company
data.description:
UBS downgraded a few steel companies on Thursday, with Steel Dynamics (NASDAQ:STLD) and Nucor (NYSE:NUE) downgraded to Neutral, and Commercial Metals Company (NYSE:CMC) to Sell.

The analysts downgraded STLD, as it expects the stock to take a "breather after recent outperformance".

"We believe the stock is unlikely to re-rate in the near term more than the current ~9x 2025E EV/EBITDA estimates in a demand environment that remains stubbornly soft with potential downside risk to 1Q25/FY25 consensus estimates," UBS said in a note.

UBS sees a recovery in demand next year and lower imports, but also believes it is partly priced in already when significant uncertainty still remains.

Early in November, steel company stocks had rallied on the belief that a second Donald Trump presidency will be better for the group than the outgoing Biden administration.

SA quant rates the company at Hold, while SA analysts rate it at Strong Buy.

Shares in the company were up 13.1% since the start of the year.

UBS downgraded Nucor (NUE) on weaker earnings.

"The 3Q24 report and 4Q24 guide negatively surprised us and we see ~27% downside risk to consensus EBITDA in 1Q25 & ~16% in 2025," the analysts said.

UBS expects product margins to stay pressured as high priced backlogs roll of and also anticipates plate to be a drag on earnings.

Plate has been particularly challenged driven by soft infrastructure spend, high rates and wind delays/cancelations, UBS said.

Nucor (NUE) had reported better than expected Q3 adjusted earnings that nevertheless plunged from a year ago, while the steelmaker had said it expects another drop in profit for Q4, with sales prices and volumes likely to fall further.

SA quant rates it at Hold, while SA analyst at Buy. Shares in the company were down 20.5% since the start of the year.

Shares down 1.1% in premarket trade.

UBS also downgraded CMC to Sell after "recent rally and court case loss."

"CMC has held onto more of its post election gains, a result

we see as surprising given CMC announced on election day that it is liable for >$330m to PSG after losing a court case," the analysts said.

"We still expect rebar pricing to recover in 2025 on improving demand and greater import protection but the stock prices this in at ~7.3x NTM EV/EBITDA estimates despite ongoing weak demand and potential downside to 1Q25 consensus estimates."

The analysts added the shares price in U.S. shipments rising more than the planned 15% capacity growth by 2026, but UBS sees risks for the shares "if tariffs do not materialise or demand disappoints following the recent rise in the yield curve."

"After weak 3Q24 results & soft 4Q24 guides across the sector, as well as soft demand & pricing, consensus expectations have moved in the opposite direction," UBS said.

SA quant and SA analysts rate the firm at Hold. Shares in the company were up 23.1% since the start of the year.

Shares down 2.6% in premarket trade.

data.title: Emera, New Jersey Resources upgraded at J.P. Morgan
data.description:
• Emera (TSX:EMA:CA) and New Jersey Resources (NYSE:NJR) on Thursday were upgraded by analysts at financial-services firm J.P. Morgan. They also resumed research coverage of Algonquin Power & Utilities (AQN:CA).
  • Emera (EMA:CA) was upgraded to Neutral from a previous investment rating of Underweight.
  • New Jersey Resources (NJR) was upgraded to Overweight from Neutral previously.
  • Algonquin Power (AQN:CA) coverage was resumed at Neutral.

data.title: Kodiak Gas Services announces stock sale by holders
data.description:
• Kodiak Gas Services (NYSE:KGS) has announced an offering of 5.5M shares of its common stock by an affiliate of the funds known as EQT Infrastructure III and EQT Infrastructure IV.
  • Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC and J.P. Morgan are acting as the underwriters for the offering.
  • Kodiak will not sell any shares of its common stock in the offering and will not receive any proceeds from the sale.
  • KGS shares dipped 2.16% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: Google's new chips, AI capabilities, could help cloud, ad businesses: BofA
data.description:
The launch of Google's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) new tensor processing units and artificial intelligence models could help boost the tech giant's cloud and advertising businesses, Bank of America said on Thursday.

Shares of the tech giant slipped fractionally in premarket trading. However, they have gained more than 10% over the past five trading sessions, due in part to the announcement of its new quantum computing chip.

"Google’s proprietary new TPUs could be a differentiator for Cloud sales and will also help reduce internal compute costs," Bank of America analyst Justin Post, who has a Buy rating and $210 price target on Alphabet, wrote in a note.

The new Trillium chips — which were announced in May — are now generally available and offer four times the performance and are two-thirds more energy efficient than the previous TPUs, Google said.

In addition, the launch of its Gemini 2.0 Flash experimental artificial intelligence model should help the company's ad business by improving search and targeting, Post said. There's also the potential for new subscription offerings and enterprise offerings on the cloud side, thanks to the new models, he added.

"We acknowledge potential negative catalysts (ChatGPT product rollouts and potential ad tier, adverse remedies in Search case, or ruling in ad-tech case) but think Street could be underestimating potential upside from AI next year (AI Overviews, agents, cloud demand, etc.)," Post wrote in the note.
